name: Rangaramanuja Muni
dates_notes: Late sixteenth to mid-seventeenth century; pupil of Pañcamabhañjana Tāta-deśika and Vatsya Anantācārya; dated by manuscript chronology and gurupāramparā lists. No firm civil records are extant.
core_thesis: Raṅgarāmānuja Muni, honoured in his school as *Upaniṣad-Bhāṣyakāra*, is the first ācārya of the Viśiṣṭādvaita tradition to compose Sanskrit *bhāṣyas* on the principal Upaniṣads — a corpus Rāmānuja himself never wrote. Working in the post-Vedānta-Deśika Vaḍakalai milieu, he produced commentaries on the Kena, Kaṭha, Praśna, Muṇḍaka, Māṇḍūkya, Taittirīya, Chāndogya, Bṛhadāraṇyaka, Mantrika, Kauṣītaki, Śvetāśvatara, and Mahā-Nārāyaṇa Upaniṣads, fitting the Upaniṣadic teaching at each contested locus to the *śarīra-śarīrin* template Rāmānuja had established for the *Brahma-Sūtra*. The exegetical method is consistent across the corpus: identity-predications are read by the *ghaṭaka-śruti* method, *neti-neti* is read as fixing inexhaustibility rather than unreality, *antaryāmin*-passages are read as the inner-controller relation. His *Viṣaya-Vākya-Dīpikā* glosses the Upaniṣadic *vākyas* quoted in the *Śrī-Bhāṣya*; his *Śārīraka-Śāstra-Dīpikā* compresses Rāmānuja's full *sūtra*-system; his *Siddhānta-Sāra* states the school's tenets compendiously. The work fills the most conspicuous gap in the school's pre-modern *bhāṣya*-coverage.

engaged_works.title: Upanishad-Bhashyas (twelve principal Upanishads)
engaged_works.composition_dates_notes: Composed across his mature scholarly period.
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored bundle of Sanskrit bhāṣyas on twelve principal Upaniṣads.

ascription_evidence.description: Stable Vaḍakalai manuscript tradition; printed editions Madras and Mysore presses, late nineteenth and early twentieth century.

engaged_works.summary: Sanskrit *bhāṣyas* on twelve principal Upaniṣads — Kena, Kaṭha, Praśna, Muṇḍaka, Māṇḍūkya, Taittirīya, Chāndogya, Bṛhadāraṇyaka, Mantrika, Kauṣītaki, Śvetāśvatara, and Mahā-Nārāyaṇa. Bundled here as a single work-entry; sub-loci are the per-Upaniṣad commentaries. The interpretive method applies the *śarīra-śarīrin* doctrine across the corpus. The Chāndogya commentary handles the *Sad-Vidyā* and the *tat tvam asi*-locus by the *ghaṭaka-śruti* method, against the standard Advaitin reading of *jahad-ajahal-lakṣaṇā*. The Bṛhadāraṇyaka commentary, the longest of the set, treats the *antaryāmin-brāhmaṇa* and the *Maitreyī-brāhmaṇa* as the locus classicus for the inner-controller relation. The Māṇḍūkya commentary, against the Gauḍapāda-Śaṅkara reading, is constructed to defend the realist Viśiṣṭādvaita ontology of the four *avasthā*-s. The Kena commentary takes up the question of the Upaniṣad's allegory of Brahman appearing to Indra, Agni, and Vāyu. The work fills the bhāṣya-coverage gap of the Viśiṣṭādvaita school, which had Rāmānuja's *Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya* and *Gītā-Bhāṣya* but no comprehensive Upaniṣadic commentaries.

engaged_works.title: Vishaya-Vakya-Dipika
engaged_works.summary: A Sanskrit *ṭīkā* glossing the *viṣaya-vākyas* — the Upaniṣadic passages quoted at each *adhikaraṇa* of Rāmānuja's *Śrī-Bhāṣya* as the topic-establishing scriptural lemmas. The work is the natural complement to Raṅgarāmānuja's Upaniṣad-bhāṣyas: the latter give the Upaniṣadic passages their place within the source-Upaniṣad's economy; the *Viṣaya-Vākya-Dīpikā* gives the same passages their place within the *sūtra*-bhāṣya economy. The result is a doubled exegetical apparatus that lets the school read each contested *adhikaraṇa* through both the Upaniṣadic context and the *sūtra*-bhāṣya argument. The work has been heavily relied on by later Vaḍakalai commentators, including Vīrarāghavācārya, and supplies the philological warrant for the school's reading at every disputed locus where the Advaitin and Viśiṣṭādvaitin readings diverge on the same Upaniṣadic *vākya*. The doubled apparatus is doctrinally consequential because it lets the school hold the *bhāṣya*-argument together with the Upaniṣadic context without subordinating either to the other.

engaged_works.title: Shruta-Prakashika-Bhava-Prakashika
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored sub-commentary on Sudarśana Sūri's Śruta-Prakāśikā.
engaged_works.summary: A Sanskrit sub-commentary on Sudarśana Sūri's *Śruta-Prakāśikā*, itself the school's standard sub-commentary on Rāmānuja's *Śrī-Bhāṣya*. Raṅgarāmānuja's work is therefore a third-order commentary: *bhāṣya* (Rāmānuja) → first sub-commentary (Sudarśana) → second sub-commentary (Raṅgarāmānuja). The function is to make Sudarśana's compressed dialectic readable for a later pāṭhaśālā audience and to update the polemical front against post-Sudarśana Advaita reformulations, especially the developments through Sarvajñātman, Citsukha, Prakāśātman's Vivaraṇa-line, and the early modern Madhusūdana-line. The work is a structural exemplar of how the Vaḍakalai pāṭhaśālā tradition transmits its core texts: each generation receives the *bhāṣya* through layered commentary, and a substantial portion of the school's modern teaching curriculum runs through Raṅgarāmānuja's third-order gloss rather than directly through Sudarśana's first-order one.

engaged_works.title: Nyaya-Siddhanjana-Vyakhya
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored commentary on Vedānta-Deśika's Nyāya-Siddhāñjana.
engaged_works.summary: A Sanskrit commentary on Vedānta-Deśika's *Nyāya-Siddhāñjana*, the school's compendium of Viśiṣṭādvaita-compatible *padārtha*-theory. Raṅgarāmānuja's gloss extends Deśika's category-scheme into a fuller dialectic with the post-Deśika Navya-Nyāya environment. Where Deśika fixed the school's *padārtha*-list and the doctrine of *apṛthak-siddhi*, Raṅgarāmānuja addresses post-Deśika Naiyāyika challenges to those positions, and supplies the modern argumentative resources the school needed by the seventeenth century. The treatment of *dharma-bhūta-jñāna* is doctrinally consequential: the school's distinctive thesis that the *jīva*'s constitutive attribute is substance-awareness rather than awareness *simpliciter* receives a sustained defense against the standard Naiyāyika contention that this doctrine collapses into either a *guṇa*-account or a *kriyā*-account. The commentary is part of the school's seventeenth-century scholastic infrastructure and is read alongside the *Śārīraka-Śāstra-Dīpikā*.

engaged_works.title: Shariraka-Shastra-Dipika
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored compendium of Rāmānuja's sūtra-system.
engaged_works.summary: A Sanskrit compendium that compresses Rāmānuja's full *sūtra*-system into a single readable treatise. The work proceeds *adhikaraṇa* by *adhikaraṇa* through the *Brahma-Sūtra* in the order of Rāmānuja's *Śrī-Bhāṣya* but at greatly reduced length, fixing each *adhikaraṇa*'s topic, *pūrvapakṣa*, *siddhānta*, and Viśiṣṭādvaita doctrinal payoff. The function is pedagogical: where the *Śrī-Bhāṣya* is the school's primary reference and the *Śruta-Prakāśikā* its standard sub-commentary, the *Śārīraka-Śāstra-Dīpikā* is the entry-point for advanced students approaching the system as a whole. The compression preserves the school's load-bearing argumentative steps — the *sapta-vidhā-anupapatti* against Advaita, the *antaryāmin*-locus argument for *śarīra-śarīrin*, the *ghaṭaka-śruti* mediation of identity-predication — and supplies the modern student the topology of Rāmānuja's argument before they engage the text in detail.

engaged_works.title: Siddhanta-Sara
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored compendium of Viśiṣṭādvaita tenets.
engaged_works.summary: Raṅgarāmānuja's one independent Sanskrit *prakaraṇa*. The work states the school's tenets — Brahman as Nārāyaṇa-with-Śrī, the *cit* / *acit* / *Īśvara* triad, the body-soul relation as *apṛthak-siddhi*, the three-*pramāṇa* epistemology, the *bhakti-yoga* path — in compendious form. The treatise is shorter than the *Śārīraka-Śāstra-Dīpikā* and is constructed not as a *sūtra*-by-*sūtra* compression but as a topical exposition. It functions as the school's modern handbook in the seventeenth-century Vaḍakalai pāṭhaśālā tradition. The treatment of *upāya* is broadly Vaḍakalai-aligned but treats the *prapatti*-*bhakti-yoga* distinction with more economy than Vedānta-Deśika himself, in keeping with the *prakaraṇa* genre. The work is read alongside Raṅgarāmānuja's commentaries as the school's compact entry-point and supplies the modern student with a single text covering the system's full topical range.
